text: All the tribes of Israel came to David at Hebron and said they were his own flesh and bone.
They reminded him that even while Saul was king, he was the one who led Israel out and brought them in, and that the LORD had said he would shepherd and rule the people.
So all the elders of Israel came to the king at Hebron; David made a covenant with them before the LORD, and they anointed David king over Israel.
David was thirty years old when he began to reign, and he reigned forty years.
In Hebron he reigned over Judah seven years and six months, and in Jerusalem he reigned over all Israel and Judah thirty-three years.
David and his men marched to Jerusalem against the Jebusites, who told David he would not get in, saying even the blind and the lame could keep him out.
But David captured the stronghold of Zion — that is, the City of David.
That day David said whoever attacked the Jebusites should go up through the water shaft; so David and his men took the city.
David lived in the stronghold and called it the City of David, and he built it up all around, from the Millo inward.
David became greater and greater, for the LORD God of hosts was with him.
Hiram king of Tyre sent messengers to David with cedar trees, carpenters, and masons, and they built David a house.
When the Philistines heard David had been anointed king over Israel, they went up in full force to search for him, but David went down to the stronghold.
The Philistines came and spread out in the Valley of Rephaim.
David asked the LORD if he should attack the Philistines, and the LORD told him to go up, for he would surely give them into his hand.
David came to Baal-perazim and defeated them there, saying the LORD had broken through his enemies before him like a bursting flood; so the place was called Baal-perazim.
The Philistines abandoned their idols there, and David and his men carried them away.
The Philistines came up once more and spread out in the Valley of Rephaim.
David inquired of the LORD, who told him not to go straight up but to circle around behind them and come at them from near the balsam trees.
When he heard the sound of marching in the tops of the balsam trees, he was to move quickly, for the LORD would have gone out ahead of him to strike the Philistine army.
David did as the LORD commanded, and struck down the Philistines all the way from Geba to Gezer.
